ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. ICC certificate with security code and QR verification.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. Single-page invoice from Forest Papper AB.

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number is SEG-26D-465042 which does not contain any LC-related banking terminology. No fields labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar were identified.

search_results.location: Invoice issuer address block - top of page 1

consignor_search_step2b.conclusion: Consignor name 'Forest Papper AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice as the issuer/branding at the top of the page, matching the certificate's consignor.

pairing_validation_8_6.reasoning: Criterion 2 met: Certificate consignor 'Forest Papper AB' is identified in the invoice as the issuer (header branding 'FOREST PAPPER AB' and address 'Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG'). However, the certificate references invoice number 111512 while the invoice bears number 111465. The certificate consignee 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' in Istanbul, Turkey does not appear in the invoice (which names 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' in Izmir, Turkey). Despite criterion 2 being met, significant mismatches exist in invoice number and consignee. Proceeding to full checkpoint verification.

document_pairing.pairing_method: Consignor match (criterion 2). Certificate consignor 'Forest Papper AB' matches invoice issuer 'FOREST PAPPER AB'. Note: Certificate references invoice 111512 but invoice bears number 111465 — this is a mismatch that will be assessed in checkpoint verification.
document_pairing.notes: The certificate references invoice 111512 dated 10.02.2026, but the invoice in the invoice file is number 111465 dated 2026-01-20. Different order numbers as well (cert: 19815, invoice: 19757). The consignee in the certificate (KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS, Istanbul) differs entirely from the buyer/delivery address in the invoice (Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S, Izmir). This strongly suggests these documents do not belong to the same transaction.

candidates.raw_text: KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS
candidates.normalized_text: kütahya entegre kagit san ve tic.as

candidates.raw_text: MERDIVENKÖY MAH. DIKYOL SK B BLOK NO 2 IC KAPI NO 9, KADIKOY, ISTANBUL
candidates.normalized_text: merdivenköy mah. dikyol sk b blok no 2 ic kapi no 9, kadikoy, istanbul

candidates.raw_text: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S
candidates.normalized_text: oral ofset amb. san. ve tic. a.s

candidates.raw_text: Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY
candidates.normalized_text: çapak mah. 2561. sok. no:23, torbali, izmir, turkey

candidates.raw_text: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S
candidates.normalized_text: oral ofset amb. san. ve tic. a.s

consignor.certificate_value: Forest Papper AB, Åsgatan 4, 41463, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN
consignor.invoice_value: FOREST PAPPER AB, Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG, VAT no: SE556878121401

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Forest Papper AB' found as 'FOREST PAPPER AB' in invoice header/branding
MATCH after normalization of case. 'Forest Papper AB' = 'FOREST PAPPER AB'
MATCH - VAT number prefix 'SE' in SE556878121401 confirms Sweden. Address also confirms Göteborg, Sweden.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure - consignor name found in invoice header/branding
Country verification via VAT prefix SE → Sweden confirmed

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Forest Papper AB' at Åsgatan 4, 41463, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN. The invoice is issued by 'FOREST PAPPER AB' at Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG with VAT no SE556878121401. After case normalization, the names are identical. The address matches (minor format difference in postal code 41463 vs 414 63 is trivial). Country Sweden is confirmed via VAT prefix SE and address. Rule 4.1.0 gives MATCH as the consignor name is explicitly found in the invoice.

consignee.certificate_value: KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS, MERDIVENKÖY MAH. DIKYOL SK B BLOK NO 2 IC KAPI NO 9, KADIKOY, ISTANBUL, Türkiye
consignee.invoice_value: Buyer: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S, Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY; Delivery address: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S (same)

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee can be identified in invoice Buyer field
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Invoice Buyer is 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' in Izmir. Certificate consignee is 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' in Istanbul. These are entirely different companies.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee appears in delivery address
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Delivery address is also 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' in Izmir. Certificate consignee not found.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 'KÜTAHYA' or 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE' anywhere in invoice
comparison_steps.result: NOT FOUND - The name 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Verify if this could be a name variation
comparison_steps.result: NOT APPLICABLE - The names 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' and 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' share no common words except generic Turkish business suffixes. These are entirely different legal entities.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification - Buyer field has priority
Consignee name verification - certificate consignee cannot be identified in invoice
Name matching boundaries - entirely different company names, not a variation

evidence.raw_text: KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS, MERDIVENKÖY MAH. DIKYOL SK B BLOK NO 2 IC KAPI NO 9, KADIKOY, ISTANBUL
Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S, Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY
evidence.relevance: Invoice buyer - different company from certificate consignee

evidence.raw_text: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S, Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY
evidence.relevance: Delivery address - same as buyer, not matching certificate consignee

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' located in KADIKOY, ISTANBUL, Türkiye. The invoice Buyer field (primary factoring field per 4.2.0.2) is 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' at Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY. The Delivery address is also 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' at the same Izmir address. The certificate consignee 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT' does not appear ANYWHERE in the invoice. These are completely different companies (different names, different cities - Istanbul vs Izmir). While both are in Turkey, the company names share no identity-bearing words. Per 4.1.3.1 boundary rules, this is a clear MISMATCH as the consignee cannot be identified in the invoice. No corporate structure link exists per 4.2.0.1. Sequential rules 4.1.3.2–4.1.3.7 are inapplicable as the names have no common core.

goods_description.certificate_value: 126 Packages Kraftliner, Invoice Date and Number : 10.02.2026 / 111512
goods_description.invoice_value: Description of Goods: TESTLINER & FLUTING, Invoice no: 111465, Invoice date: 2026-01-20

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are specified in certificate
comparison_steps.result: NOT APPLICABLE - No article numbers specified in certificate

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 - Priority 3: Generic goods with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains invoice reference (111512, 10.02.2026). Checking if reference matches invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Certificate references invoice 111512 dated 10.02.2026. Invoice bears number 111465 dated 2026-01-20. Neither the invoice number nor the date match.

comparison_steps.description: Identity-bearing main designation: 'Kraftliner'. Search in invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Invoice describes goods as 'TESTLINER & FLUTING'. The term 'Kraftliner' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. 'Testliner' and 'Kraftliner' are different paper products. 'Fluting' is also a different paper product. No article numbers or codes match either.

comparison_steps.description: Check if invoice uses abbreviations for Kraftliner
comparison_steps.result: NOT APPLICABLE - Invoice uses codes RF1, T3, THP which do not correspond to Kraftliner. RF1 likely refers to Recycled Fluting, T3 to Testliner, THP to unknown. None of these are abbreviations for Kraftliner.

rules_applied.description: Generic goods with invoice reference - invoice number 111512 does not match invoice 111465; date 10.02.2026 does not match 2026-01-20
Controlled text matching - 'Kraftliner' not found in invoice which describes 'TESTLINER & FLUTING'

evidence.relevance: Invoice number and date - does not match certificate reference
Invoice goods description - different products from certificate

goods_description.debug_explanation: The certificate describes goods as '126 Packages Kraftliner' and references invoice 111512 dated 10.02.2026. The invoice in the document pair is number 111465 dated 2026-01-20. The invoice number mismatch (111512 vs 111465) and date mismatch (10.02.2026 vs 2026-01-20) mean the generic goods with invoice reference rule (4.3.5) fails. Additionally, the identity-bearing main designation 'Kraftliner' does not appear in the invoice at all. The invoice describes 'TESTLINER & FLUTING' with product codes RF1, T3, and THP. Kraftliner, Testliner, and Fluting are distinct paper/board products — they are not synonyms or variations. This is a clear MISMATCH on goods description. The order numbers also differ (cert: 19815, invoice: 19757), further confirming these are different transactions.

quantity.certificate_value: 325987 KG (box 7); Gross and Net Weight : 325987 KG (box 6)

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains '325987 KG'. This is the verification-grounding quantity.

comparison_steps.result: NOT FOUND - The value 325987 does not appear anywhere in the invoice. Invoice total quantity is 24,938 (which represents 24.938 MT or 24938 KG).

comparison_steps.description: Convert invoice total 24.938 MT to KG: 24.938 × 1000 = 24938 KG. Compare with certificate 325987 KG.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - 24938 KG ≠ 325987 KG. Difference is massive (factor ~13x).

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value (325987 KG) is the verification-grounding quantity
Direct occurrence check - 325987 not found in invoice
Exception 3B MT↔KG conversion - 24.938 MT = 24938 KG ≠ 325987 KG

evidence.relevance: Invoice total quantity - 24.938 MT = 24938 KG, vastly different from certificate's 325987 KG

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states 325987 KG. Certificate box 6 also states 'Gross and Net Weight : 325987 KG'. The invoice's total quantity is 24,938 (which from the line items, each in MT, sums to 24.938 MT = 24,938 KG). The certificate's quantity of 325,987 KG is approximately 13 times greater than the invoice's 24,938 KG. No conversion, summation, or normalization can reconcile these values. This confirms these documents relate to entirely different shipments. Clear MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice

comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Austria ≠ Germany. These are different countries.

comparison_steps.description: Both Austria and Germany are EU member states. However, the certificate specifically names 'Austria' (not 'EU') and the invoice specifically names 'Germany'. Per 4.5.5, each origin country in the certificate must be explicitly identifiable in the invoice. 'Austria' cannot be identified in the invoice which states 'Germany'.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - Certificate says Austria, invoice says Germany. Both are EU members but the certificate names a specific country. Per 4.5.4.2 rule about specific named EU countries: if cert says a specific EU country, invoice must confirm that specific country or say 'EU'. Invoice says 'Germany' not 'Austria' or 'EU'.

rules_applied.description: Origin identification - invoice explicitly states 'Goods origin Germany'
EU member country normalization - Certificate says 'Austria', invoice says 'Germany'. Neither says 'EU'. These are two different specific EU countries. Austria ≠ Germany.
Positive verification requirement - 'Austria' cannot be identified in invoice which only states 'Germany'

evidence.relevance: Invoice origin - different from certificate origin

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states origin as 'Austria'. Invoice explicitly states 'Goods origin Germany'. These are two different countries. While both are EU member states, the certificate specifically names Austria (not 'EU' or 'European Union'). Per rule 4.5.4.2, the EU↔member state equivalence applies when one document says 'EU' and the other says a specific member state. Here, BOTH documents name specific — but different — EU member states. Per 4.5.5, the certificate's origin 'Austria' must be explicitly identifiable in the invoice. The invoice says 'Germany', not 'Austria'. Therefore MISMATCH. Rule 4.5.4.2 does NOT make all EU member states interchangeable with each other — it only makes 'EU' interchangeable with any specific member state.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: NOT ACTIVATED - No LC reference found in certificate
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate number SEG-26D-465042 does not constitute an LC reference. No banking-term context found.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: PAIRED with concerns - Consignor matches (Forest Papper AB) but invoice number, consignee, goods, and quantities all differ between certificate and invoice, strongly suggesting wrong invoice was attached.
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate refs invoice 111512 but invoice file contains invoice 111465. Certificate consignee is KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE in Istanbul, invoice buyer is Oral Ofset in Izmir.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Invoice reference in certificate vs invoice file
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: NOTED - Certificate references invoice 111512 but invoice file contains invoice 111465. Per 8.5.2 this should result in MANUAL_REVIEW. However, the full checkpoint verification reveals multiple MISMATCH results beyond just the invoice number, confirming NOT_IDENTICAL as the stronger and more appropriate result.
rule_evaluation_log.details: The invoice number mismatch is one of multiple fundamental discrepancies.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: PASSED (criterion 2) - Consignor identified in invoice. However, consignee and invoice number do not match. Proceeding to full verification which reveals fundamental mismatches.
rule_evaluation_log.details: Only criterion 2 (consignor) met. Criteria 1 (invoice number) and 3 (consignee) both failed.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH - Forest Papper AB found as FOREST PAPPER AB in invoice header
rule_evaluation_log.details: Name and address match, country confirmed via VAT prefix SE.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MISMATCH - Invoice Buyer field contains 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' which is an entirely different company from certificate consignee 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS'
rule_evaluation_log.details: Different companies, different cities (Istanbul vs Izmir), no shared identity-bearing words.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: NOT APPLICABLE - Certificate consignee does not appear in any invoice field
rule_evaluation_log.details: KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE not found anywhere in invoice.
Both the invoice number and date referenced in certificate do not match the invoice.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MISMATCH - 'Kraftliner' not found in invoice; invoice describes 'TESTLINER & FLUTING'
MISMATCH - 325987 KG not found in invoice; invoice total is ~24938 KG
MISMATCH - Certificate says Austria, invoice says Germany
rule_evaluation_log.details: Different specific EU member states. EU↔member state rule not applicable as neither document says 'EU'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice might contain KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE as an alternate buyer or notify party
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Full text search of the invoice confirms 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE' does not appear anywhere in the document. The only buyer/delivery party is 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Kraftliner might be a general term encompassing Testliner and Fluting
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Kraftliner, Testliner, and Fluting are distinct paper/board products in the paper industry. Per rule 4.3.3, synonymy and semantic interpretation are not allowed. The identity-bearing main designation 'Kraftliner' does not appear in the invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Austria and Germany are both EU members so origin might match
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.5.4.2, EU↔member state equivalence only applies when one document says 'EU'/'European Union' and the other names a specific member state. Here both documents name different specific member states. Austria ≠ Germany.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice 111465 might be a related/replacement invoice for certificate's referenced invoice 111512
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 8.5.2, if the certificate references invoice 111512 but the invoice file contains 111465, this is a fundamental mismatch. The system may not assume these are related invoices. Additionally, every verifiable datapoint differs: consignee, goods, quantity, origin, order number, and date.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: This should be MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 8.5.2 since the referenced invoice is not in the file
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: While 8.5.2 would normally give MANUAL_REVIEW for a missing referenced invoice, the full checkpoint verification reveals multiple definitive MISMATCH results (consignee, goods, quantity, origin). Per the consolidation rules in Step 6, if at least one critical checkpoint is MISMATCH, comparison_result must be NOT_IDENTICAL. The evidence overwhelmingly shows these documents do not belong together.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet och fakturan avser helt olika transaktioner. Certifikatets mottagare är KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT i Istanbul medan fakturan är ställd till Oral Ofset i Izmir. Certifikatet beskriver Kraftliner med en vikt på 325 987 kg och ursprung Österrike, medan fakturan avser Testliner & Fluting med en totalvikt på ca 24 938 kg och ursprung Tyskland. Fakturanumret i certifikatet (111512) stämmer inte heller med fakturans nummer (111465).
summary_of_mismatches: Consignee: Certificate names 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' (Istanbul) but invoice buyer is 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' (Izmir) - entirely different company
Goods description: Certificate describes 'Kraftliner' but invoice describes 'TESTLINER & FLUTING' - different products
Invoice reference: Certificate references invoice 111512 dated 10.02.2026 but invoice is number 111465 dated 2026-01-20
Order number: Certificate references order 19815 but invoice references order 19757
Quantity: Certificate states 325,987 KG but invoice total is approximately 24,938 KG (~13x difference)
Country of origin: Certificate states Austria but invoice states Germany
blocking_issues: Wrong invoice attached - the certificate references a completely different transaction than the provided invoice

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Forest Papper AB, Åsgatan 4, 41463, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN
sections.invoice_value: FOREST PAPPER AB, Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG, VAT no: SE556878121401
sections.rule_applied: Corporate structure rule - consignor name found in invoice header/branding; country confirmed via VAT prefix SE
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Forest Papper AB' identifieras som 'FOREST PAPPER AB' i fakturans sidhuvud. Adressen 'Åsgatan 4, Göteborg' överensstämmer. Landet Sverige bekräftas via VAT-nummer med prefix SE. MATCH.

sections.certificate_value: KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS, MERDIVENKÖY MAH. DIKYOL SK B BLOK NO 2 IC KAPI NO 9, KADIKOY, ISTANBUL, Türkiye
sections.invoice_value: Buyer: Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S, Çapak Mah. 2561. Sok. No:23, Torbali, IZMIR, TURKEY
sections.rule_applied: Priority order for consignee identification - certificate consignee cannot be identified anywhere in invoice
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'KÜTAHYA ENTEGRE KAGIT SAN ve TIC.AS' i Istanbul förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Fakturans köpare är 'Oral Ofset Amb. San. Ve Tic. A.S' i Izmir — ett helt annat företag i en annan stad. Inga gemensamma identitetsbärande ord. MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: 126 Packages Kraftliner; Invoice Date and Number : 10.02.2026 / 111512
sections.invoice_value: Description of Goods: TESTLINER & FLUTING; Invoice no: 111465; Invoice date: 2026-01-20
sections.rule_applied: Generic goods with invoice reference failed (wrong invoice number and date); Controlled text matching failed ('Kraftliner' not found in invoice)
sections.motivation: Certifikatet hänvisar till faktura 111512 daterad 10.02.2026 men den bifogade fakturan har nummer 111465 daterad 2026-01-20. Varken fakturanummer eller datum överensstämmer. Dessutom beskriver certifikatet varan som 'Kraftliner' medan fakturan anger 'TESTLINER & FLUTING' — helt olika pappersprodukter. MISMATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Total Quantity 24,938 (≈24938 KG baserat på radposter i MT)
sections.rule_applied: Direct occurrence check - 325987 not found in invoice; conversion confirms massive discrepancy
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 325 987 KG. Fakturans totalkvantitet är 24,938 MT (= ca 24 938 KG). Skillnaden är ungefär 13 gånger. Värdet 325 987 förekommer inte i fakturan. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Positive verification requirement - Austria not identifiable in invoice which states Germany
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger ursprungsland Österrike (Austria). Fakturan anger 'Goods origin Germany'. Österrike och Tyskland är olika länder. EU-normaliseringsregeln är inte tillämplig eftersom inget dokument anger 'EU' — båda namnger specifika men olika medlemsstater. MISMATCH.

human_readable_report.overall_result: NOT_IDENTICAL — Fyra av fem kontrollpunkter visar MISMATCH. Certifikatet och fakturan avser helt olika transaktioner (olika mottagare, olika varor, olika kvantitet, olika ursprungsland, olika fakturanummer). Trolig orsak: fel faktura har bifogats.

output_quality_checks.notes: 4 out of 5 checkpoints are MISMATCH. Consistency verified: multiple MISMATCH → comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL → workflow = MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED. This appears to be a case where the wrong invoice was attached to the certificate application.
